title: Graph Bipartite Check
hints: Try assigning a color to each node as you traverse the graph. What happens if a neighbor already has the same color?
Use BFS or DFS to visit every node. Maintain an array to store the color (e.g., 0 and 1) of each visited node.
Start from any unvisited node, color it, and recursively check its neighbors. If a conflict arises, the graph is not bipartite.
